Musician, environmental lobbyist
[Sampler](/wiki/Sampler_(musical_instrument) "Sampler (musical instrument)"), vocals, percussion, [synthesizer](/wiki/Synthesizer "Synthesizer"), [hurdy gurdy](/wiki/Hurdy_gurdy "Hurdy gurdy")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American musician

| Geologist | |
| --- | --- |
| [Geologist performing live](/wiki/File:Geologist.png "Geologist performing live") Geologist performing live | |
| Background information | |
| Born | Brian Ross Weitz[[1]](#cite_note-1)   (1979-03-26) March 26, 1979 (age 46)  [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore"), [Maryland](/wiki/Maryland "Maryland"), U.S. |
| Occupations | Musician, environmental lobbyist |
| Instruments | [Sampler](/wiki/Sampler_(musical_instrument) "Sampler (musical instrument)"), vocals, percussion, [synthesizer](/wiki/Synthesizer "Synthesizer"), [hurdy gurdy](/wiki/Hurdy_gurdy "Hurdy gurdy") |

Musical artist

**Brian Ross Weitz** ([/ˈweɪts/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*WAYTS*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) born March 26, 1979), also known by his stage name **Geologist**, is an American musician best known as a member of the [experimental pop](/wiki/Experimental_pop "Experimental pop") group [Animal Collective](/wiki/Animal_Collective "Animal Collective"). He provides electronic sound manipulations and [samples](/wiki/Sampling_(music) "Sampling (music)") for the band.

Weitz grew up in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia") and [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore")[[3]](#cite_note-newgay-3) and lives in [Washington, DC](/wiki/Washington,_DC "Washington, DC").[[4]](#cite_note-4) His nickname comes from a friend mistaking his [major in college](/wiki/College_major "College major"), as well as the [headlamp](/wiki/Headlamp "Headlamp") he wears in order to see his electronic equipment during live shows.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Animal Collective

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geologist_(musician)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Animal Collective")]

Geologist attended the [Park School of Baltimore](/wiki/Park_School_of_Baltimore "Park School of Baltimore") where he met future Animal Collective bandmates [David Portner](/wiki/Avey_Tare "Avey Tare") (aka Avey Tare) and [Josh Dibb](/wiki/Deakin_(musician) "Deakin (musician)") (aka Deakin). Avey Tare, Geologist and Deakin first started an indie-rock band called "Automine" with two other schoolmates. Deakin introduced his childhood friend [Noah Lennox](/wiki/Panda_Bear_(musician) "Panda Bear (musician)") (aka Panda Bear) to Avey Tare and Geologist. Throughout their high school years and winter/summer college breaks, the four of them played music in different variations and often solo, swapping homemade recordings and sharing ideas.

Geologist went on to study at [Columbia University](/wiki/Columbia_University "Columbia University"), while Avey Tare went to [NYU](/wiki/NYU "NYU"), Panda Bear to [Boston University](/wiki/Boston_University "Boston University"), and Deakin to [Brandeis University](/wiki/Brandeis_University "Brandeis University"). Avey Tare recorded tracks with Panda Bear which eventually became *[Spirit They're Gone, Spirit They've Vanished](/wiki/Spirit_They%27re_Gone,_Spirit_They%27ve_Vanished "Spirit They're Gone, Spirit They've Vanished"),* which was released under "Avey Tare and Panda Bear" in August 2000. After Panda Bear and Deakin both left school and moved to New York in 2000, the group's music became more collaborative, and Avey and Panda began playing clubs around New York. Geologist soon began performing with the group who eventually became "Animal Collective." He first appeared on their 2001 release *[Danse Manatee](/wiki/Danse_Manatee "Danse Manatee")*, and has contributed to every Animal Collective recording to date, except for their 2004 *[Sung Tongs](/wiki/Sung_Tongs "Sung Tongs")* LP and their 2003 *[Campfire Songs](/wiki/Campfire_Songs_(Animal_Collective_album) "Campfire Songs (Animal Collective album)")* LP (though he was present during the recording of the latter to operate the MiniDisc recorders). Before that time, Geologist had gone to Arizona for graduate school.[[6]](#cite_note-pbsanimal-6)

When asked about his personal musical influences, he says "I was always inspired by horror soundtracks, things that use unconventional sounds and textures as music. I'm interested in how abstract sounds can have the same impact on the listener as traditional musical sounds.[[3]](#cite_note-newgay-3)"

## Other musical projects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geologist_(musician)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Other musical projects")]

As well as performing various DJ sets, Geologist has released the track "Jailhouse" on Animal Collective's *Keep Cassette*, "Stretching Songs for Spring" on a digital split with Avey Tare entitled *New Psycho Actives Vol. 1* and created the soundtrack for the Morphologic Studios short film *Man O War*.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) In April of 2018 he released the limited edition cassette *Live in the Land of the Sky* - his first proper solo release. [[9]](#cite_note-9)

Geologist collaborated with Doug Shaw (credited as "D.S.") on the album *A Shaw Deal*, released in January 2025.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The singles "Loose Gravel" and "Route 9 Falls" were released on January 13, 2025 ahead of the album.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

He also hosts a monthly radio show on [NTS Radio](/wiki/NTS_Radio "NTS Radio") called *The O'Brien System*.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geologist_(musician)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In addition to his musical career, Weitz also worked on ocean conservation and environmental policy. He has a BA from Columbia University in Environmental Science, and an [MPA](/wiki/Master_of_Public_Administration "Master of Public Administration") in Environmental Policy from Columbia University's School of International and Public Affairs.[[13]](#cite_note-twosea-13)

Weitz was married on [Halloween](/wiki/Halloween "Halloween") in 2009.[[14]](#cite_note-14) He and his wife had a son in August 2010.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Musical equipment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geologist_(musician)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Musical equipment")]

* [MiniDisc](/wiki/MiniDisc "MiniDisc") – sample playback[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* [Kaoss Pad](/wiki/Kaoss_Pad "Kaoss Pad") – effects[[17]](#cite_note-auto-17)
* [Roland SP-555](/wiki/Roland_SP-555 "Roland SP-555") – [sampler](/wiki/Sampler_(musical_instrument) "Sampler (musical instrument)")[[17]](#cite_note-auto-17)
* [Roland SP-404](/wiki/Roland_SP-404 "Roland SP-404") – sampler[[17]](#cite_note-auto-17)
* Moog Taurus 3 Pedals[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* [Moog Sub 37](/wiki/Moog_Sub_37 "Moog Sub 37")[[17]](#cite_note-auto-17)
* [Moog Little Phatty](/wiki/Moog_Little_Phatty "Moog Little Phatty")[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geologist_(musician)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Live in the Land of the Sky* (2018)
* *A Shaw Deal* (2025) (with D.S.)
* *Can I Get a Pack of Camel Lights?* (2026)
